fields.name: menstrual
fields.description: The uterine lining sheds. Estrogen and progesterone are at their lowest. Bleeding occurs and prostaglandins drive contractions.

fields.name: follicular
fields.description: Estrogen rises steadily as follicles develop in the ovaries. Energy and mood improve. Overlaps with menstruation in the first few days.

fields.name: ovulation
fields.description: A surge in LH triggers the release of a mature egg from the dominant follicle. Estrogen and testosterone peak simultaneously.

fields.name: luteal
fields.description: The corpus luteum produces progesterone after ovulation. If no pregnancy occurs, it degenerates and the cycle restarts.

fields.name: late
fields.description: The period is overdue. Could indicate pregnancy, stress, illness, or cycle irregularity. Progesterone may be sustained or disrupted.
dominant_hormones: uncertain — sustained progesterone or hormonal disruption
